title: DmofwHi Cordless Coffee Grinder Electric, USB Rechargeable Coffee Bean Grinder with 304 Stainless Steel Blade and Removable Bowl-Black
description:
About this item
【Cordless & Portable Coffee Grinder】~DmofwHi electric coffee grinder is designed to be cordless, and USB rechargeable. The cordless coffee grinder comes with a large capacity for repeated usage. A battery indicator helps you check the battery whenever you grind. Put the coffee bean grinder into your suitcase, and enjoy the quality coffee when traveling.
【Powerful & Evenly Grinding】~ The powerful 200W motor of DmofwHi's small camping coffee grinder let your every-time grinding finer and more evenly. A 170ml removable grinding bowl can provide you with enough ground coffee powder to get 12 cups of coffee at one time.
【Grind without Splashing】~The portable coffee grinder comes with an anti-splash lid that effectively prevents the coffee powder from splashing everywhere when working. Pour the beans into the bowl of the coffee bean grinder, cover the lid, press to activate grinding, and you will quickly get an evenly ground coffee. You can monitor the desired coffee powder as you like, based on 6 seconds for coarse powder, 10 seconds for medium powder, and 15 seconds for fine powder.
【Detachable Grind Bowl for Quick Cleaning】~The electric mini coffee grinder comes with a food-grade 304 stainless steel grinding blade and removable bowls that have the advantage of material safety, durability, and superior grinding performance. The detachable cup can be removed from the base for directly rinsing. A specialized brush helps you thoroughly clean the powder that let the cleaning easier for you.
【Grind Quietly in the Morning】~The compact coffee grinder equips with a low noise motor, that is quieter than most traditional electric coffee grinders on the market. You don’t have to worry create too much noise and disturbing your family when grinding fresh ground coffee in the early morning.
reviews_5s:
I ordered DmofwHi Cordless Coffee Grinder and love that is cordless. It's sleek and can be taken to trips, etc. I like the short bursts and it makes perfect grind for variety of press. The charge seems like stays for awhile. I grind dry herbs into powder. I did grind espresso also. The little bowl comes off easily to clean. I was worried might change the coffee taste, but there was zero strange taste to my coffee afterwards. It is very compact. I leave it on my counter all the time, but I could easily fit it into a cabinet. It's quiet too. My electric kettle is louder, not this one. And I recommend it.I have written very few 5-star reviews but this is probably the most surprising product I have purchased on Amazon. I ordered and received this in mid May. I fully charged it upon receiving it. it is now almost the end of October. I have used it every single day for the past six months and I am still only down to 50% battery used. Now, like a lot of battery indicators that may fall to zero tomorrow, but at this point I'm amazed. I bought this grinder because I wanted to save counter space and outlet usage. I don't have to have it stored on the counter and I don't have to plug/unplug it every day to store it. It just keeps going...and going...and going. I'm at the point where I think someone else is secretly charging this SOB behind my back but I am the only person who drinks coffee in our packed house - hence I don't want to take up space with something only I use. I've been putting off writing this until it reaches zero percent but at this stage that might not ever happen and I may be dead. And you know what - if it at some point loses the ability to charge - if I have to ever charge it again - I have already gotten my money's worth out of it since it is one less thing I have to frickin charge all the time.

TL;DR buy this thing because 1) it may outlive you and you will rarely have to charge it and 2) it works great.No more cord on kitchen counter! Still a bit messy when removing cap but easy to handle. Don't fill beans to top line as they won't get ground. Sound is normal, not annoying. Recharge may be needed after every few uses. Easy to remove cup and rinse carefully. Not sure how long blade will last fastener looks a whole lot flimsier than my 25 year old Proctor Silex model. Appearance is truly elegant, I'd say this is a "go".I ordered this to grind coffee for my French press pretty much every morning. I got the cordless because it's sleek and I thought I would take it camping too. I can use short bursts and it makes perfect grind for French press. I've had it FOUR months and I haven't had to charge it once.

I grew hot peppers that I dried and used this to grind them into powder, so I know it can do espresso grind too. The little bowl comes off easily to clean. I was worried, but there was zero pepper taste to my coffee afterwards.

It's slightly bigger than a coffee tumbler, but still very compact. I leave it on my counter all the time, but I could easily fit it into a cabinet. It's quiet too. My electric kettle is louder tbh.Much quieter than the 120V coffee grinder we have. Works obviously much better while camping, being a 12V version. Has amazingly not needed a charge since I purchased it 2 1/2 months ago. And grinds coffee just fine. Easy to dump the grinds into the coffee maker with the removal bowl.I’ve used mine every day since it arrived, i charged it with the supplied USB-C cable when i first opened it and haven’t had to charge it since. (It’s been about a month). It’s a great little grinder, simple to use and easy to clean. - the stainless steel cup twists to unlock and then just wash with soap and water.

To grind your coffee, just put the beans in, put the lid on and then the top cover goes over, push down gently and it’ll grind the beans. There aren’t any options for the type of grind but, it’s easy enough to take the lid off and look in between pulses. I have also used this at home and it’s fairly quiet so you won’t wake anyone up!

Overall, this is a fantastic product, well worth the money if you’re looking for a compact grinder that travels easily.Stir in a drop of water on your finger tip to dampen the beans and eliminate the static electricity. I use twice a day and wash the bowl once a week with water. Quiet and lasts months on a charge using 5 seconds and 20 grams coffee for French press and Aeropress.I’ve had this for over a year I don’t use it every day seems to hold charge for long periods of time and the best part. You’re not restrained by an outlet. Has a nice chic look nice weight to it.Currently on a camping trip as I write this. It’s works wonderfully.Funciona tal y como me lo imaginé
reviews_1s:
Update 5 months after purchase:

I now realize it’s greatest flaw is that there’s no way to turn it off if it’s malfunctioning. Today it started to act strange and turned itself on multiple times throughout the day. The noise is so loud and unbearable. I couldn’t figure out how to stop the buzzing. Currently I’ve wrapped it in blanket and placed it in a tightly sealed box so that the buzzing is reduced.

Has anyone had similar issues? It should still work well for the intended purpose but the buzzing is so annoying.

——— original review ———
It’s been a while since I wrote another review. This grinder really made me happy :)

I did quite a few minutes of research among the hundreds of varieties of coffee grinders out there and eventually picked this one, and it did not fail my expectations.

1) The cordless feature is perfect for either using at home or office or traveling. Charge it at home and use it without worrying!

2) You can remove the container completely and since it’s stainless steel you can wash it in whichever way you like (some other brands are non-removable and would be such a hassle)

3) Yes it will get hot after grinding for a while, but that’s the same for other grinders. I personally would use frozen coffee beans (best for freshness IMO) and the container would actually stay cold, although I would still follow the safety instructions:)

4) Coffee bean, spice, nuts, oats… I’ve used it for many recipes and they all work perfectly! I am obsessed with grinding my own spice for grilled meat now (freshly ground cumin is so much more flavorful than store bought ground cumin!)
5) Compact. I did not want another equipment to take over my kitchen counter space.

Overall 10/10I purchased this coffee grinder 2.5 years ago and really liked the cordless feature. After 2.5 years of not even daily use, the machine will not recharge. I am giving it only one star based on this failure.Had this grinder for about 2 1/2 years, using it periodically. Hadn’t used it in 6 months and when I went to use it, I got the flashing 20% indicator. Charged it for 4+ hours until 100% showed. Afterward, when I tried to use it, it went back to flashing 20% and won’t function at all. From the customer reviews, this is not an uncommon issue. I wouldn’t recommend this grinder in all good conscience. I’ve replaced it with corded product so hopefully there’ll be no battery related issues. Durability gets a 0 score.I originally gave this 5 stars but now I have to downgrade to 1 star because there isn't a rating any lower. I used it to grind my herb. Worked great. Then one day I decided to buy some TOP SHELF herb. I ground it like I normally do. About 3 taps on the lid. As the grinder was sitting on the table it just turned itself on and destroyed all the herb in it. It wouldn't shut off. Buttons wouldn't respond. It worked about as good as any other product made in CHINA and that is for a couple of months only. JUNK. Quality is a term used decades ago when you purchased a product and you had to pass it down in your will because it still worked! That was then and FOR PROFIT is now. That's all they care about. If it stops working or falls apart they are glad because you'll buy another and another and another. I'd pay $300 for one of these that you can actually depend on. Who want's to keep buying this JUNK over and over again? Make one that is dependable so those of us who are willing to purchase QUALITY can!I bought two of these Cordless Electric Coffee Grinders, both from Amazon. The first one I purchased January 8, 2022 as a gift. The recipient did not try to use it until May of 2022. AT that time it was fully charged with the included power cord but would not work at all. I confirmed it would not work at all so I still have it brand new in the box. I decided to purchase another exactly the same as the first one on July, 2023. When I received the product, I charged it fully and used it successfully until today October 14th, 2023. It appeared to need charging so I hooked up the charging cord and after several hours it read 100%. I disconnected the grinder, put the usual amount of beans in it and it ran for less than 10 seconds. I now have two, one brand new never used and one slightly used, but neither of them work. The brand website states 6-month Free Replacement so I wrote them an email.I bought this in aug/ 22 .
On arriving at our off grid location, and before arriving to our remote location, we charged this grinder up.
It will not hold a charge. It’s not possible to use it plugged in as well.
I would like to say this is not our first rechargeable coffee grinder from this company. Months earlier we bought the same one.. and we loved it! However..in our move, we dropped it.
This was the reason for the second purchase.
We are positive we simply received a bad one. Also As I went thru the trouble shooting, we could see this unit had been used before we received it. There was a abundance of white ground powder inside. Under the stainless steel removable cup.
I’m hoping that the company will stand by this non functional unit.
I do like this coffee grinder, the size, the look and the previous one that we broke…that one, it worked perfect. And the charge on the unit was very long lasting.
So if your thinking about this unit, I would say yes.
Should I hear back and find resolve to my purchase. I will come back and change my star rating.Dead in less than a year. Get a manual grinder and stop buying this planned obsolescence trash.From the looks this grinder is made well, but its weakness is the switch that turns the grinder on and off when the top piece is pressed. In the first grinder I bought, this switch stopped working in about three weeks, and I could still return it and get a replacement. That was in October, 2022. It is now May, 2023, and my second grinder just stopped working. Nothing happens when I press the top plastic piece. I suppose it is still under warranty – I have to find that out now, but really, I would rather not deal with the hassle of a warranty repair. The other problem with this grinder is, you never know how much charge is remaining. You have to operate it to see whether it is 25%, 50%, 75% or 100% charge, but this is not very reliable. It just stops working whenever it pleases, and you have to then charge it. Minor annoyance and I could live with it. But with a faulty switch, I just cannot operate the grinder at all. Avoid this brand.I’ve been using this grinder for a few months now to give it a fair shot. I can say with absolute confidence that this coffee grinder stinks. I like an espresso grind, and this is incapable of doing that. Something to do with the way the blades spin I suppose and the overall design, cause it to grind and incredibly uneven texture. The bottom of the grinding vessel gathers a super fine powder while the top is as course as drip. I cannot get this coffee grinder to grind and even espresso grind. It is, terrible.I only used it three times and now it is not working!
